text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE


Clinic Date: 21 September 2025

Dr Priya Shah
Riverside Health Centre

12 Mill Lane, Southwark, London SE1 2AB
Dear Dr Priya Shah
Sarah Collins, DOB: 14-03-1990, Hospital No: H937624 NHS No. 8421635790
Flat 3, 22 Brookfield Road, London, SE15 4JT 

Diagnoses/summary: Recurrent unprovoked seizures of uncertain classification; current control improving. Prior routine EEG reportedly non-diagnostic. No known structural diagnosis to date.

Medication history: Previously tried levetiracetam (fatigue, irritability), carbamazepine (dizziness), and clonazepam PRN for clusters. Currently on sodium valproate 500 mg twice daily; omeprazole 20 mg once daily; Vitamin D3 1000 IU daily.

I reviewed Sarah Collins in clinic today. She attended alone. She works in a distribution warehouse and reports that the peak season has recently ended; with reduced workload and overtime she feels her overall stress and sleep pattern have improved, and she has noticed better seizure control.

Seizure history/progression: She had no seizures for nearly a year following initiation of Valproate, then developed myoclonic jerks leading to 3 tonic seizure 2 Saturdays ago. She attributed the event to cumulative sleep deprivation during the final week of the busy period. There were no reported injuries. Recovery was faster than previous events, and there have been no further episodes since.

Current status: She reports improved energy, more regular sleep, and fewer prodromal symptoms. No new neurological deficits, no visual disturbances, and no medication side-effects beyond mild transient nausea at initiation which has resolved. She is not driving.

Examination: General and neurological examinations today were unremarkable. Vitals within normal limits.

Investigations: I have requested liver function tests, full blood count, and valproate trough level prior to the next visit to ensure therapeutic range and tolerability. No change to imaging at present; prior head CT (community, 2022) was normal by report.

Plan:
- Continue sodium valproate 500 mg twice daily; adherence reinforced.
- Emphasised sleep hygiene, hydration, and regular meals given recent improvement coinciding with reduced workload.
- Safety advice reviewed (bathroom precautions, machinery at work). She will continue occupational adjustments that limit overtime during peak periods where possible.
- Bloods in 6 weeks (LFTs, FBC, valproate level); results to be copied to you.
- Seizure diary to track any further myoclonic jerks or tonic events.
- Routine follow-up in 4 months, sooner if recurrence or concerns.

Overall, her condition is improving, coinciding with the end of peak season at the warehouse and reduced overtime, with patient-reported better control since workload normalised.

Thank you for your ongoing care.

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter states: "She had no seizures for nearly a year following initiation of Valproate, then developed ... 3 tonic seizure 2 Saturdays ago." This indicates that over approximately a 1-year period she experienced 3 seizures (all occurring on one day), with none before that in the preceding months. Therefore, infer a rate of 3 per year.
